requirements.text: An implementation boundary MUST identify its version, governed surfaces, accountable organization, and accountable human role.
A Kernel-governed operation MUST identify the applicable Kernel family version set and the status of each source.
A lower-layer artifact MUST NOT silently redefine a Charter, accepted architecture boundary, or authoritative source text.
A material operation MUST identify people who may be affected even when they are not account holders or interface operators.
The system MUST preserve separations among affected person, subject, speaker, representative, operator, reviewer, accountable human, accountable organization, AI component, and service provider.
Representative authority MUST be scoped, evidenced, purpose-bound, time-bounded, contestable, and revocable where applicable.
A personal-data or interpretive operation MUST record a legitimate declared purpose before collection, transformation, recommendation, disclosure, or reuse.
Where consent is the applicable authority basis, its scope, state, evidence, expiry, revocation, and exceptions MUST remain inspectable.
Refusal, silence, withdrawal, or disagreement MUST NOT be treated as failure, resistance, consent, or proof that an interpretation is correct.
A materially affected person MUST have a usable path to inspect, contest, correct, contextualize, and appeal significant interpretation or decision records.
Third-party and non-user information MUST be purpose-limited, minimized, access-controlled, and protected from unlimited representative disclosure.
Fact, observation, statement, inference, hypothesis, pattern, judgment, recommendation, and decision MUST remain distinguishable.
A significant knowledge item MUST preserve source, speaker, subject, time, context, transformation history, and applicable authority.
A significant inference MUST preserve uncertainty, limitations, unresolved alternatives, and unknowns; confidence MUST NOT be represented as truth.
Contradictions, corrections, failures, counterexamples, dissent, and superseded interpretations MUST remain traceable subject to privacy and safety controls.
The system SHOULD gather and distinguish relevant observation, context, uncertainty, and affected-person perspective before offering consequential advice.
Consequential interpretation SHOULD preserve relevant relationship, sequence, change over time, and structural or material context without assuming that context proves causation.
A proposed action SHOULD preserve meaningful choice and identify whose goal, need, and authority the action serves.
When uncertainty or impact is material, the system SHOULD prefer the smallest reasonably safe, reversible, observable action.
Before a high-impact operation, the system MUST identify affected people, foreseeable consequences, alternatives, reversibility, notice, correction, appeal, and review conditions.
The system MUST NOT use hidden persuasion, anxiety, shame, dependency, urgency, or vulnerability to obtain consent, compliance, purchase, retention, or disclosure.
A system operating in a domain with foreseeable serious harm MUST define risk recognition, uncertainty, escalation, stop, and incident-recording behavior.
Current capability, professional status, jurisdiction, service availability, limitations, and unavailable functions MUST be represented honestly.
Generating a referral, resource, message, or link MUST NOT be represented as a completed handoff unless availability and completion evidence support that claim.
A material adverse decision or failure MUST have a traceable appeal, incident, containment, review, correction, and follow-up path appropriate to its scope.
An emergency or protective intervention MUST be lawful where applicable, necessary, proportionate, purpose-limited, time-bounded where possible, reviewable, and distinct from consent.
A person materially represented in memory MUST have an applicable path to inspect, correct, contextualize, contest, and identify significant downstream effects.
Memory authority, revocation, retention, deletion, backup exceptions, access, and completion evidence MUST be represented explicitly.
Temporary distress, vulnerability, conflict, diagnosis, risk, or limitation MUST NOT become an unreviewable permanent identity or commercial profile.

Secondary use, cross-context inference, model or system improvement, research, analytics, and training use MUST have a disclosed authority basis, purpose, data boundary, retention rule, and refusal or exception path.
An AI component MUST expose its role, relevant provider or model version, capabilities, limitations, generated or inferred status, and significant provenance.
A high-impact decision MUST identify a human and organization with sufficient information, authority, independence, stop capacity, and review responsibility.
A high-impact design or decision MUST define how affected-person participation, refusal, representation, accessibility, and situations where participation is unsafe or impossible are handled.
Observed outcome, affected-person feedback, correction, failure, and new evidence MUST be able to revise relevant state without erasing prior provenance.
Material operations MUST preserve audit, version, source, decision, exception, failure, counterexample, dissent, and unknown records according to applicable privacy and safety controls.

No product, organization, model, prompt, workflow, or repository MUST be described as Kernel conforming before applicable conformance requirements, tests, evidence, review, exceptions, and claim-expiry rules exist and are satisfied.

ambiguities.issue: Does the core govern software only, or also human professional and organizational operations?
How far indirect, group, relational, future, or non-user impact extends.
When one person may hold several roles without destroying independence.
How authority varies by age, capacity, jurisdiction, urgency, and conflict.
Required granularity and when a changed purpose requires renewed authority.
When consent is inapplicable, invalid, coerced, or insufficient.
When refusal can be overridden for lawful protective action.
Which derived records and decisions must be updated after correction.
How a user can inspect their own record without exposing another person's protected information.
Boundary among observation, statement, inference, hypothesis, pattern, judgment, and decision.
When immediate safe guidance may precede complete observation.
Whether numeric confidence is meaningful across human and AI interpretation.
How unknowns and minority views affect operations rather than becoming passive notes.
How to preserve context without creating intrusive relationship surveillance.
What small, safe, and reversible mean in different domains.
Boundary between supportive communication, risk disclosure, recommendation, and manipulation.
Thresholds for impact, likelihood, reversibility, duration, scale, and vulnerable groups.
When uncertainty itself requires stopping, escalation, or human review.
Obligations when qualified human or emergency services are unavailable.
Who determines necessity and proportionality, with what evidence and review.
Conflicts among deletion, backup, audit, safety, legal, and other-person rights.
Whether and how learning from sensitive interaction can be authorized without exploitation.
What model and provenance detail is meaningful to an affected person.
Required competence, independence, information, authority, and availability.
How to proceed when participation is unsafe, inaccessible, declined, or impossible.
Which requirements apply directly to organizational policy, sales, staffing, and governance.
How referenced RFC/Profile requirements remain synchronized without copying.
How Kernel Drafts respond when a Candidate Charter is revised.
How English and Chinese sources remain semantically traceable.
Which Draft requirements eventually become mandatory, conditional, optional, or removed.

vocabulary.working_meaning: A human being; never reducible to account, role, profile, record, or model output.
A person whose rights, welfare, identity, relationship, opportunity, or safety may be materially affected.
The person, group, relationship, event, or object being described.
The source of a statement, observation, correction, or claim.
A person acting for or with another person under scoped authority.
An actor initiating or controlling an operation or interface.
A human responsible for a bounded decision or operation.
The entity responsible for governance, service, or a claim.
A model or agent performing bounded generated, inferred, or execution functions.
The declared reason and allowed scope for an operation or data use.
The documented basis that permits an action, role, or data operation.
A scoped authority state where consent is the applicable basis; not a universal basis for all action.
A time- and source-bound record of what was perceived or measured.
Content asserted by a speaker, distinct from verified fact.
A derived interpretation that remains distinguishable from source records.
A testable or revisable possible explanation.
A proposed recurrence or relationship across records, not automatic causation.
A human or system evaluation under stated criteria and authority.
A selection or authorization with consequences and accountability.
A source-linked record used to support or challenge a claim or decision.
A bounded assessment of support or uncertainty; never equivalent to truth.
A material absence, uncertainty, conflict, or unresolved question.
A retained record or derived state that can affect later operations.
A proposed or executed change with actor, target, purpose, authority, and expected effects.
Observed or reported information about an action, state, interpretation, or outcome.
A possible harmful consequence characterized by severity, likelihood, immediacy, uncertainty, and reversibility.
A suspected or confirmed harmful, unauthorized, or materially nonconforming event.
A request for review, correction, suspension, or reversal of a material decision or record.
Current evidence that a human, professional, emergency, or external resource can actually respond.
A provisional category for operations with material rights, safety, identity, relationship, opportunity, or lasting consequence.
